criteria.name: FWI reference vector tests
criteria.evidence: test_fwi_vectors.py calls fine_fuel_moisture_code, duff_moisture_code, and drought_code directly with parametrized reference vectors derived from gagreene/cffdrs. Each test asserts abs(result - expected) < 0.01 (TOLERANCE=0.01). 9 test vectors total (3 per moisture code) covering drying, wetting, and edge cases. Tests verify actual output correctness against reference implementation, not just shapes or types.

criteria.name: DMC and DC implementations
criteria.evidence: fwi.py contains all three moisture-code functions. fine_fuel_moisture_code implements full Van Wagner (1987) FFMC with rain correction and drying/wetting equilibrium. duff_moisture_code implements DMC with _DMC_LE day-length factors, log drying rate, and piecewise b-coefficient rain model. drought_code implements DC with _DC_LF day-length adjustment, potential evapotranspiration, and 2.8mm rain threshold. All pass their reference vector tests within 0.01 tolerance.

criteria.name: Imputation framework with audit trail
criteria.evidence: imputation.py provides ImputationConfig (configurable short_gap_max_hours, medium_gap_max_hours, short_gap_method, long_gap_method), AuditRecord (station, variable, time_start, time_end, method, count_affected), impute_column, impute_frame (per-station grouping), and audit_trail_to_dataframe. Tests verify: no-missing→empty audit, short gap interpolation, long gap preservation, audit record field types, ffill strategy, multi-column imputation, per-station audit, empty→DataFrame, records→DataFrame. 10 tests all pass.

criteria.name: QA/QC summary artifacts
criteria.evidence: qa_qc.py provides missingness_summary (by station+variable with missing_count, total_count, missing_pct), duplicate_timestamps (duplicate station+timestamp pair detection), out_of_range_values (configurable ranges with sensible meteorological defaults for 10 variables), and coverage_summary (by station with first/last timestamp, expected_hourly, coverage_pct). Tests use synthetic data with planted anomalies (temp=-70, rh=110, rain=-1) and verify detection. Edge cases covered: no missing, no dupes, custom ranges, missing station column raises ValueError.

criteria.name: Loader handles multiple schema families
criteria.evidence: normalized_loader.py correctly routes to _parse_timestamp_date_time for minimal_date_time_family, hoboware_date_time_family, and legacy_dual_wind_family, and to _parse_timestamp_single for single_timestamp_family. Unsupported families raise clear NotImplementedError. However, test_normalized_loader.py only tests ONE family (minimal_date_time_family via Cavendish CSV). The spec requires 'tests cover at least 2 families.' Real data for hoboware_date_time_family (e.g. PEINP_GR_WeatherStn_Dec2022.csv) and legacy_dual_wind_family (e.g. PEINP_GR_WeatherStn_Feb2024.csv) exists in data/raw/peinp/ but is not exercised in the loader test. Manifest tests cover schema recognition for 2+ families, but not the loader's timestamp parsing path.

criteria.name: Explore notebook automated verification
criteria.evidence: test_explore_smoke.py has 3 tests: (1) test_notebook_file_exists checks notebooks/01_explore.ipynb exists, (2) test_notebook_is_valid_json verifies it parses as nbformat v4 with a Python kernelspec, (3) test_notebook_executes_without_error uses nbconvert ExecutePreprocessor with 120s timeout to run the entire notebook end-to-end on cleaned data. This goes beyond the spec minimum ('executable or at least importable') — it does full notebook execution.

summary: REJECT — AC-REM-5 fails. normalized_loader.py correctly implements multi-family routing with NotImplementedError for unsupported families, but test_normalized_loader.py only exercises one schema family (minimal_date_time_family). The spec explicitly requires tests covering at least 2 families. Real data for hoboware_date_time_family and legacy_dual_wind_family exists in data/raw/peinp/ but no loader test uses it. Fix: add at least one more test in test_normalized_loader.py that loads a CSV from a different schema family (e.g. a George River hoboware CSV) and verifies it parses correctly with the right timestamp and schema_family label. All other ACs (REM-1 through REM-4, REM-6) pass — implementations are substantive with real reference vectors, proper audit trails, and full notebook execution testing.
